channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Vladimir Putin Hasn't Looked This Desperate In Over 25 Years
item.description: As the Russian position weakens, there is possibility of a coup against Putin, for whom just too many things have gone wrong in the last four years.
item.content:encoded: As the Russian position weakens, there is possibility of a coup against Putin, for whom just too many things have gone wrong in the last four years.

item.title: Astra Missiles, BrahMos Battery Defence Deals In PM Modi's Indonesia Visit

media:content.media:description: A Su-30 jet with Astra missiles attached to it

item.description: In a big endorsement of India's election management model, India will also support the development of Indonesia-specific EVMs, sources said.
item.content:encoded: In a big endorsement of India's election management model, India will also support the development of Indonesia-specific EVMs, sources said.

item.title: Florida Influencer DreamDoll Brii, 21, Killed While Driving Her Lamborghini

media:content.media:description: DreamDoll Brii was inside her vehicle when a white sedan pulled up next to the vehicle and opened fire

item.description: The 21-year-old influencer, whose real name was Brianna Johnson, died after a shooting in Miramar, Florida.
item.content:encoded: The 21-year-old influencer, whose real name was Brianna Johnson, died after a shooting in Miramar, Florida.

item.title: Trump's Friend Urges Him To Declare "National Emergency" Ahead Of Midterms

media:content.media:description: Peter Ticktin calls for emergency declaration citing foreign interference in US elections.

item.description: Ticktin, who says he has been friends with Trump since their days at the New York Military Academy, believes that there could be foreign intelligence through electronic voting machines.
item.content:encoded: Ticktin, who says he has been friends with Trump since their days at the New York Military Academy, believes that there could be foreign intelligence through electronic voting machines.

item.title: Pakistan's "War" Threat To India Over Indus Waters Amid Monsoon Blues

media:content.media:description: Islamabad's reaction to India's action has been frantic.

item.description: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto Zardari also warned that Pakistan was prepared to fight "on all fronts" over the Indus waters.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto Zardari also warned that Pakistan was prepared to fight "on all fronts" over the Indus waters.

item.title: Ex-Chinese Official Sentenced To Death For Taking $325 Million In Bribes Over 30 Years

media:content.media:description: Yang Youlin also was convicted of embezzlement, offering bribes, abuse of power and money laundering

item.description: China sentenced former official Yang Youlin to death for taking $325M in bribes over 3 decades, marking a major escalation in its anti-graft drive.
item.content:encoded: China sentenced former official Yang Youlin to death for taking $325M in bribes over 3 decades, marking a major escalation in its anti-graft drive.

item.title: US Man Sues ICE, Says Home Visit Over Email Violated Free Speech Rights

media:content.media:description: Representatives for ICE previously declined to comment on the warning to Streever (Representational)

item.description: David Streever sued ICE after agents targeted his home over an email criticizing leadership, sparking a major First Amendment legal battle.
item.content:encoded: David Streever sued ICE after agents targeted his home over an email criticizing leadership, sparking a major First Amendment legal battle.

item.title: Over 300 Children Killed Or Wounded In Sudan War Over 6 Months: UNICEF

media:content.media:description: The conflict has killed at least 59,000 people and displaced some 13 million

item.description: UNICEF reports over 300 children killed or wounded in Sudan's civil war over six months, with drone strikes causing 60% of casualties nationwide
item.content:encoded: UNICEF reports over 300 children killed or wounded in Sudan's civil war over six months, with drone strikes causing 60% of casualties nationwide

item.title: Storms Kill 8 In China As Typhoon Bavi Looms Offshore

media:content.media:description: Growing climate-driven weather extremes threaten billions of dollars in annual economic losses in China

item.description: At least eight dead as thunderstorms with 149 kph winds batter Hubei Province; torrential rain and Super Typhoon Bavi threaten millions across China
item.content:encoded: At least eight dead as thunderstorms with 149 kph winds batter Hubei Province; torrential rain and Super Typhoon Bavi threaten millions across China

item.title: US Cybersecurity Agency Uses Anthropic AI Model To Audit Government Software

media:content.media:description: Anthropic has had a tumultuous relationship with the US government

item.description: CISA is using Anthropic's Mythos AI to scan government code repositories for security bugs, uncovering many vulnerabilities amid ongoing tensions
item.content:encoded: CISA is using Anthropic's Mythos AI to scan government code repositories for security bugs, uncovering many vulnerabilities amid ongoing tensions

item.title: Biggest Hamas Decision In 20 Years Leaves One Critical Question Unanswered

media:content.media:description: Hamas militants secure area as workers along with ICRC members search for bodies of hostages in Gaza

item.description: For almost 20 years Hamas has combined armed resistance with day-to-day administration in the Gaza Strip. It has operated ministries, schools, municipal services and health institutions while simultaneously maintaining an armed organisation.
item.content:encoded: For almost 20 years Hamas has combined armed resistance with day-to-day administration in the Gaza Strip. It has operated ministries, schools, municipal services and health institutions while simultaneously maintaining an armed organisation.

item.title: After US-Iran Deal, Pakistan Turns Peacemaker For Fractured Libya: Report

media:content.media:description: Any successful plan to reunify Libya would need to balance the interests of foreign patrons.

item.description: A summary of a proposed "Libya Reunification Plan" showed it would set out a 36-month transitional power-sharing arrangement under a body called the Government of National Consensus and Presidential Council.
item.content:encoded: A summary of a proposed "Libya Reunification Plan" showed it would set out a 36-month transitional power-sharing arrangement under a body called the Government of National Consensus and Presidential Council.

item.title: Musk No Longer A Trillionaire: What Went Wrong With SpaceX IPO

media:content.media:description: Most of Musk's fortune comes from his ownership in SpaceX.

item.description: Musk's fortune dropped below the trillion-dollar mark once again, fluctuating around $992-$997 billion depending on the wealth tracker.
item.content:encoded: Musk's fortune dropped below the trillion-dollar mark once again, fluctuating around $992-$997 billion depending on the wealth tracker.

item.title: Oil Tanker Hit By Unknown Missile In Strait Of Hormuz: UK Maritime Agency

media:content.media:description: A tanker has reported being hit by an unknown projectile on the port side, the Maritime Agency said.

item.description: An "unknown projectile" struck and caused a fire on an oil tanker off the coast of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, British maritime security agency UKMTO said.
item.content:encoded: An "unknown projectile" struck and caused a fire on an oil tanker off the coast of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, British maritime security agency UKMTO said.

item.title: US Concerned By China Nuclear Buildup After Missile Launch

media:content.media:description: Analysts said that the test demonstrated growing Chinese capacity to strike the mainland of the US.

item.description: The US said Monday that China's "rapid and opaque" nuclear weapons buildup "is of great concern" after Beijing test-fired of a missile from a submarine into the Pacific Ocean.
item.content:encoded: The US said Monday that China's "rapid and opaque" nuclear weapons buildup "is of great concern" after Beijing test-fired of a missile from a submarine into the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Ukraine War Resolution "Closer" After Talks With Putin, Zelensky: Trump

media:content.media:description: Trump said that he spoke to Putin and Zelensky at the weekend to end Ukraine war.

item.description: US President Donald Trump said on Monday that a resolution to the more than four-year-old war in Ukraine is "getting closer than people realize" and that he will talk about Ukraine during talks in Turkey this week at a NATO summit.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump said on Monday that a resolution to the more than four-year-old war in Ukraine is "getting closer than people realize" and that he will talk about Ukraine during talks in Turkey this week at a NATO summit.

item.title: US Court Rejects Elon Musk Bid To Set Aside Twitter Fraud Verdict

media:content.media:description: Investors accused Musk of falsely suggesting that Twitter was overrun by fake and spam accounts.

item.description: Investors accused Musk of falsely suggesting that Twitter was overrun by fake and spam accounts, known as bots, to force the company to renegotiate his offer or let him back out. Th
item.content:encoded: Investors accused Musk of falsely suggesting that Twitter was overrun by fake and spam accounts, known as bots, to force the company to renegotiate his offer or let him back out. Th

item.title: Island-Wide Blackout Hits Cuba As Its Fuel Reserve Dwindles

media:content.media:description: An island-wide blackout hit Cuba as fuel reserves dwindle and its electric grid continues to crumble.

item.description: Cuba produces only 40% of the fuel it needs, while the 730,000 barrels of oil delivered by a Russian tanker in late March ran out by the end of April.
item.content:encoded: Cuba produces only 40% of the fuel it needs, while the 730,000 barrels of oil delivered by a Russian tanker in late March ran out by the end of April.

item.title: Trump's Fireworks Made Washington World's Most Polluted City

media:content.media:description: A fireworks display is seen during the Independence Day celebration in Washington on July 5.

item.description: July 4 fell during a record-breaking heat wave that upended the parades, block parties and barbecues that traditionally mark the day the United States celebrates its break from Britain.
item.content:encoded: July 4 fell during a record-breaking heat wave that upended the parades, block parties and barbecues that traditionally mark the day the United States celebrates its break from Britain.

item.title: Trump's "Finish The Job" Warning To Iran Amid Peace Talks

media:content.media:description: Trump claimed that the US could destroy Iran's electricity and power generation facilities within hours.

item.description: Donald Trump on Monday said the United States would either reach a deal with Iran or "finish the job", while asserting that Washington has the capability to cripple Tehran's critical infrastructure if it chooses to do so.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump on Monday said the United States would either reach a deal with Iran or "finish the job", while asserting that Washington has the capability to cripple Tehran's critical infrastructure if it chooses to do so.
